idea-plugin.name: PxPeek - CSS Pixel Hints
idea-plugin.description:
Shows the pixel equivalent of relative CSS size values as comment-style inlay hints.

Supports CSS, SCSS, SASS, and LESS.

Choose where hints appear:
  • End of line (default) — aggregated after the semicolon:
    width: min(42rem, 100%); /* 672px, 16px */
  • Inline — next to each value:
    width: min(42rem /* 672px */, 100% /* 16px */)
Configure in Settings → Editor → Inlay Hints → PxPeek.

Converts rem, em, %, pt, pc, in, cm, mm, ch, ex, vw, vh, vmin, vmax, and their small/large/dynamic viewport variants.

Defaults: 16px root font, 1440×900 viewport.

Pairs well with: CSS Variables Assistant — autocomplete, docs, and resolution tracing for var(--name).
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.3.0

  • New: Optional one-time feedback prompt — after ~14 days of real use, a single non-intrusive balloon asks if you'd like to rate PxPeek on Marketplace. Includes "Remind me later" and "Don't show again" actions.
  • New: Pairs well with CSS Variables Assistant — a sibling plugin by the same author adding autocomplete + docs for CSS custom properties.

0.2.0

  • New: Hint placement preference — choose between End of line (aggregated after the semicolon) or Inline (next to each value).
  • New: Settings panel at Settings → Editor → Inlay Hints → PxPeek.
  • Default placement changed to End of line for cleaner stylesheet readability.

0.1.0

  • First release.
  • Inline pixel-equivalent inlay hints for 18 relative CSS units.
  • Supports CSS, SCSS, SASS, and LESS.
